comment_text.sn19.9:1.9: Sūcaka appears only here in Pali. His title echoes his punishment by needles (sūci) which worm their way in and out of his body just as he, as an “informant” (Arthaśāstra 2.8.29) wormed his way through society. Manu says his punishment was to be reborn with a stinking nose (Manu 11.50). As noted in the next Sutta, the informant reported to the “village head”.

root_text.sn19.9:1.1: “Idhāhaṁ, āvuso, gijjhakūṭā pabbatā orohanto addasaṁ sūcilomaṁ purisaṁ vehāsaṁ gacchantaṁ.
root_text.sn19.9:1.2: Tassa tā sūciyo sīse pavisitvā mukhato nikkhamanti;
root_text.sn19.9:1.9: eso, bhikkhave, satto imasmiṁyeva rājagahe sūcako ahosi …pe….

translation_text.sn19.9:1.1: “Just now, reverend, as I was descending from Vulture’s Peak Mountain I saw a man whose body hairs were needles flying through the air.
translation_text.sn19.9:1.2: The needles bored into his head and out his mouth,
translation_text.sn19.9:1.3: into his mouth and out his chest,
translation_text.sn19.9:1.4: into his chest and out his belly,
translation_text.sn19.9:1.5: into his belly and out his thighs,
translation_text.sn19.9:1.6: into his thighs and out his calves,
translation_text.sn19.9:1.7: and into his calves and out his feet.
translation_text.sn19.9:1.9: “That being used to be an informant right here in Rājagaha. …”
